column47: Lack of TPU advancement, and IDEX
column49:
1) Bambu messed up with and after the H2D, and Prusa was slow to respond. None of Bambu printers starting with H2D did anything Prusa couldn’t quickly mod the XL to do without the heavy investment of new printer design, and Prusa missed that opportunity. 

2) Bambu is bad for TPU, and TPU will get its revenge. The printers are designed with it as an afterthought, and all the other companies clone that design. The AMS and TPU profiles make using TPU a bad experience, and while technically possible, Bambu makes it harder than necessary, and maintains the scary hard to print mindset for all the noobs Bambu pulls in. INDEX will kill Bambu because it is compatible with TPU.

column49:
I'm very grateful for a company like Prusa, trying to be as transparant as possible, I appreciate the advances Bambu makes on the technology and ease of use. Also the cheaper brands making a 3D printer that makes 3D printing more affordable and easier to get in to. I do think it's sad that the very big industrial companies are patenting things that shouldn't be because they can and no one is able to protest due to their size. 
Good luck and looking forward to the results!
